name: applied_mappings_no_overlap_not_enforced
code:
proof fn phi_4_applied_mappings_no_overlap_not_enforced(s: crate::os::State, base1: nat, base2: nat)
    requires
        s.applied_mappings().dom().contains(base1),
        s.applied_mappings().dom().contains(base2),
        base1 != base2,
    ensures
        base1 + s.applied_mappings()[base1].frame.size <= base2
        || base2 + s.applied_mappings()[base2].frame.size <= base1,
{
}
reason: `no_overlaps` is only a precondition of `interp_vmem_subrange` but never enforced as an invariant of `applied_mappings` — overlapping mappings could exist in the OS state

name: vmem_unmapped_is_zero
code:
proof fn phi_5_vmem_unmapped_is_zero(s: crate::os::State, c: crate::os::Constants, vaddr: nat)
    requires
        vaddr < MAX_BASE,
        !crate::os::State::has_base_and_pte_for_vaddr(s.applied_mappings(), vaddr as int),
    ensures
        s.interp_vmem(c)[vaddr as int] == 0,
{
}
reason: Unmapped virtual addresses silently return 0 rather than being undefined or causing an error — could mask bugs where unmapped memory is accidentally read as valid zero data
